title: Kids Screen Time by Age 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: WHO Screen Time Recommendations by Age
description: WHO-recommended daily screen time by age for children. Free calculator with guidelines from newborn to teens.
intro: Healthy child development requires thoughtful management of screen time. The WHO provides age-specific screen time recommendations across every developmental stage—from infants through adolescence. These guidelines help parents balance digital exposure with activities that support sleep, development, language skills, and social growth. Our calculator reflects current 2026 WHO guidelines and is reviewed regularly.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **WHO 2019 guidelines**. Direct calculation based on age. Get recommended daily screen time for your child by age group.
useCases: Parents setting daily screen time limits for their children
Health researchers and students studying digital wellness in children
Quick reference for parenting discussions and family decisions

fields.label: Child's Age
fields.help: Enter your child's current age in years.

explanation:
**Screen time management** is a critical part of healthy child development and family wellness. Different age groups have distinct developmental needs, milestones, and appropriate recommendations.

## How It Works

This calculator applies WHO age-based screen time guidelines. Enter your child's age and receive the recommended maximum daily screen time, plus helpful alternatives and important health considerations.

## Key Health Effects

Excessive screen time is linked to sleep disruption, obesity risk, delayed language development, and social-emotional challenges. Balance screen exposure with physical activity, outdoor play, and face-to-face interaction.

## Important Notes

This is a reference tool based on WHO guidelines. For personalized recommendations specific to your child's health and development, consult your pediatrician or child development specialist. All guidelines are current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What screen time is recommended for babies under 1 year?
faq.a: No screen time is recommended. Avoid all screens for children under 1 year old.

faq.q: How much screen time for toddlers ages 1-2?
faq.a: Minimize screen time. Avoid if possible; video calls with family are an acceptable exception.

faq.q: What is recommended screen time for preschoolers ages 2-5?
faq.a: Less than 1 hour per day of high-quality content. Co-view with your child when possible.

faq.q: How much screen time for school-age children ages 6-12?
faq.a: Less than 2 hours per day of non-school-related screen time. Choose quality, age-appropriate programs.

faq.q: What about screen time for teenagers?
faq.a: Model moderate usage with clear family guidelines. Prioritize sleep, physical activity, and offline social time.

faq.q: What health effects does excessive screen time cause?
faq.a: Too much screen time is linked to sleep disruption, obesity, delayed language development, and social-emotional difficulties.

faq.q: Do video calls with family count toward screen time limits?
faq.a: No. Video calls with family members typically don't count as recreational 'screen time' per WHO guidelines.

faq.q: Is this calculator free to use?
faq.a: Yes. All Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free with no registration required.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology
